summary: Third pass converting fighter code from direct `HSD_GObj` field access or `getFighter` calls to the `GET_FIGHTER` macro, with related cleanup in `fighter.c` and introduction of a `GET_JOBJ` macro for typed joint-object access. The PR touched fighter core code plus Samus and Ness files, mostly preserving matching-sensitive unused-stack padding while regularizing accessor style. It depended on PR #712 and partially superseded PR #614.
what_changed: Replaced several `getFighter(fighter_gobj)` and direct `fighter_gobj->user_data` uses with `GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj)` in `src/melee/ft/fighter.c`.
Changed direct `fighter_gobj->hsd_obj` access and casts through `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` to a new `GET_JOBJ(fighter_gobj)` macro in fighter code.
Changed `HSD_GObjGetUserData` and `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` return types from `void*` to `any_t` in `gobj.h`, and normalized the `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` parameter name to `gobj`.
Inlined the previously separate `Fighter_InitScale` helper back into `Fighter_UpdateModelScale`, using `GET_FIGHTER` and `GET_JOBJ` there.

Removed or localized temporary variables in `Fighter_UnkInitLoad_80068914`, including replacing a local `controller_index` with direct `fp->x61A_controller_index` access and replacing local `ftDataList` with direct `gFtDataList[...]` access.
Simplified zero-vector assignments in `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC` by assigning `0.0F` directly instead of introducing a local float constant.
Localized `Vec3 vec` inside the branch where it is used in `Fighter_8006A360`.
Replaced a previous matching workaround using a void-pointer downcast with the expression `!fp;` in `Fighter_8006A360`.

Added blank-line spacing around `MUST_MATCH` unused stack declarations and variable declarations in Samus and Ness functions; these changes appear style/matching-preservation oriented rather than behavioral.
Some Samus functions still use lowercase `getFighter` in the shown diff excerpt, so this was not a complete global conversion.
smart_moves: Centralizing fighter userdata access through `GET_FIGHTER` reduces repeated casts and makes future fighter decomp code more uniform.
Adding `GET_JOBJ` mirrors the fighter accessor pattern for `HSD_JObj` retrieval, avoiding repeated explicit casts from `hsd_obj` or `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj`.
The diff preserves `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack arrays while adjusting surrounding declarations, showing care not to break known stack-shape requirements during cleanup.
Inlining `Fighter_InitScale` into `Fighter_UpdateModelScale` likely reduced artificial helper indirection while keeping the scale logic explicit at the call site.
Moving temporary declarations closer to use, such as the color pointer and `Vec3 vec`, cleaned up decompiled C without changing the visible control flow.
The PR was staged as 'pass 3' and explicitly depended on #712, suggesting an incremental approach to broad mechanical accessor cleanup instead of one risky all-at-once rewrite.
decomp_lessons: When an object field is semantically a typed fighter pointer, prefer the project macro `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` over direct `gobj->user_data` access or ad hoc `getFighter` calls.
For joint-object access from an `HSD_GObj`, prefer `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` once available instead of open-coded casts from `hsd_obj`.
Accessor macro migrations can be done in matching-safe slices; leave known `MUST_MATCH` stack padding in place until the underlying stack issue is understood.
Small declaration-order and temporary-variable changes can matter for matching. The diff repeatedly keeps unused stack declarations and inserts spacing around them rather than deleting them.

Use typed accessor macros to express intent, but verify each replacement against matching because comments in the removed code indicate some previous casts/workarounds existed specifically to satisfy the compiler output.
If a cleanup only partially converts old names, make follow-up searches for remaining lowercase `getFighter` calls in the touched files.
naming_conventions: `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` is the preferred uppercase macro form for retrieving `Fighter*` from an `HSD_GObj*`.
`GET_JOBJ(gobj)` was introduced as the uppercase macro form for retrieving an `HSD_JObj*` from an `HSD_GObj*`.
Getter-like inline functions in `gobj.h` use `HSD_GObjGetUserData` and `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj`, with parameter name normalized to `gobj`.
Doxygen-style comments such as `/// @warning` and `/// @todo` are used in touched code, replacing or aligning older plain comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Kept explicit `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused byte arrays such as `u8 unused[8]`, `u8 unused[20]`, `u8 unused[24]`, and `u8 unused[32]` in Samus/Ness functions.
Used direct `0.0F` chained assignments instead of a local `float c = 0.0f` in `Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC`, implying this form matched or was cleaner under the compiler.
Replaced an old void-pointer downcast filler workaround with `!fp;`, an expression likely intended to influence codegen without affecting runtime behavior.
Localized variables to narrower scopes where possible, but not when `MUST_MATCH` stack padding needed to remain visible in the function body.
Removed the separate inline `Fighter_InitScale` helper and expressed scale setup directly in `Fighter_UpdateModelScale`, which may avoid inlining/codegen fragility.
Blank lines were inserted around unused-stack blocks in multiple functions, making matching scaffolding easier to see and less likely to be accidentally mixed with semantic declarations.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states: "Depends on #712" and "Partially supersedes #614", which is the main contextual feedback/evidence available.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining lowercase `getFighter(` calls after PR #713, especially in Samus files where the excerpt still shows some.
Search for direct `->user_data` access in fighter-related code and evaluate whether `GET_FIGHTER` or another typed accessor should be used.
Search for direct `->hsd_obj` access and decide whether `GET_JOBJ` is appropriate or whether the object is not an `HSD_JObj`.
Audit `gobj.h` for the duplicated `func_80390ED0(HSD_GObj* gobj, u32 mask);` declaration shown in the diff excerpt.
Compare with PR #712 and PR #614 to understand the full staged `GET_FIGHTER` migration and any earlier review constraints.
